tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mildly elevated creatinine, hyperchloremia, low CO₂, and abnormal urinalysis with proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, and low specific gravity, suggesting possible renal impairment and metabolic disturbance. HbA1c is unexpectedly low despite glucosuria, which may indicate a recent onset or fluctuating glucose levels. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may suggest underlying renal or hepatic pathology. Clinical correlation and further evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
